fact: April 3 is exactly 93 days into the year, which means there are exactly 272 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, cherry blossom trees are typically exploding with pink flowers around this time, creating natural confetti storms when the wind blows!
Robins often seem extra active around this time because they're in full nest-building mode and will attack their own reflection in car mirrors thinking it's a rival bird!
The sun rises almost exactly due east and sets almost exactly due west around April 3, making it super easy to find directions without a compass!
Earthworms are usually having their busiest season right now in temperate climates - the soil is finally warm enough for them to wiggle up to the surface and munch on decomposing leaves!
April 3 falls in the middle of Aries season, when the zodiac sign associated with energy and determination is at its peak!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, this date marks the heart of autumn when leaves are typically turning brilliant orange and red!
Dandelions are secretly preparing their most annoying trick right now in northern climates - growing their deep taproots up to 10 inches underground so they'll be impossible to pull out later!
The daylight is growing by about 2-3 minutes every single day in northern places around this time, which means kids get to play outside longer each evening!
Maple trees in North America are often pumping sugary sap like crazy right now - some trees can produce up to 10 gallons of sap per day for making maple syrup!
April 3 is roughly two weeks after the spring equinox, which means the Earth has tilted just enough for flowers in northern regions to start blooming everywhere!
Migrating birds are typically flying overhead in massive V-shaped formations right now in North America, with some flocks containing thousands of birds all honking and squawking together!
